claim: A core module (ontology / graph / proposal / invariant layer) shall never import a periphery module (attention, reflection, generator-internal) -- the core/periphery dependency arrow points one way only.
why: Intra-package sibling of R-core-imports-stdlib-or-hotam-spec-only (which guards the INTER-package arrow). Not yet ported in the Go port: the automated AST import-ratchet enforcer is not ported. Historically implemented as spec/tests/test_core_periphery_import_direction.py, a static AST import scan mirroring test_backend_neutral_scope.py, that proved today's core was clean plus a negative control proving the scanner was not vacuous. The Python core/periphery package split (attention.py, reflection, invariants_table_engine) does not map directly to the Go package layout (internal/ontology, internal/invariants, internal/proposal, internal/diagnose, internal/generator); the property holds structurally in the current Go dependency graph but no automated Go enforcer exists yet.
